title: The `this` Keyword

learningObjectives.text: Explain how `this` is assigned using a new example and justify the result.
Explain method calls using a new example and justify the result.
Identify and correct common `this` pitfalls using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: The `this` Keyword is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **How JavaScript Works** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **How `this` is assigned** → **Method calls** → **Common `this` pitfalls**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: How `this` is assigned
sections.body: For ordinary functions, this depends on the call form: method, constructor, explicit binding, or default binding. Arrow functions use the surrounding this instead of creating their own.

sections.title: Method calls
sections.body: In object.method(), the object before the dot provides this for that call. Detaching the method changes the call form and may lose the intended receiver.

sections.title: Common `this` pitfalls
sections.body: Common errors include passing an unbound method as a callback, expecting an arrow to receive a dynamic receiver, and assuming nested ordinary functions inherit a method's this.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The same function receives a different `this` binding because the call form changes.

commonMistakes: Assuming `this` is fixed by the definition rather than the call form.
Using an arrow where a dynamic receiver is required.
Using array indexes as keys for a reorderable list.
Expecting `key` to arrive as an ordinary prop.
summary: The `this` Keyword connects How `this` is assigned, Method calls, Common `this` pitfalls.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Why does the detached call not reliably return `Nora`?

choices.label: Methods permanently capture their first object.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The `this` Keyword rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Arrow functions automatically repair every detached method.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The `this` Keyword rule and the observable result.

choices.label: The detached function is no longer called as `account.label()`, so it does not receive `account` as `this`.
choices.feedback: The detached function is no longer called as `account.label()`, so it does not receive `account` as `this`.

choices.label: `this` is determined by where the function was written, not how it is called.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The `this` Keyword rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: The detached function is no longer called as `account.label()`, so it does not receive `account` as `this`.
questions.hint: Trace the actual values, references, call form, render cycle, request flow, or server/client boundary before choosing.

questions.prompt: Which change is the best fix?

choices.label: Copy `owner` to a global variable.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The `this` Keyword rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Replace the method with `eval('account.label()')`.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The `this` Keyword rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Preserve the receiver with `account.label.bind(account)` or call it through a wrapper such as `() => account.label()`.
choices.feedback: Preserve the receiver with `account.label.bind(account)` or call it through a wrapper such as `() => account.label()`.

choices.label: Call the detached function twice so `this` is restored.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The `this` Keyword rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Preserve the receiver with `account.label.bind(account)` or call it through a wrapper such as `() => account.label()`.
questions.hint: Choose the smallest change that fixes the underlying model, not a workaround that hides the symptom.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
